title: Misato the slut
wikitext:
[]: Misato the slut?             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Wed Feb 24, 2010 2:58 pm Although the show labels her as an overly sexual person who uses sex as escape, she's only really fucked one guy the whole time. Why didn't the series have her hooking up with other men to emphasize her character flaws rather than the one guy? The only other time was apparently her trying to get Shinji's dick wet, but other than that, it wasn't very obvious what her problem was until they state it in the end of the series. So why did the creators decide only Kaji (and Shinji) get the loving if loving is the way she copes?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Feb 24, 2010 3:11 pm That's because she's not a slut. Misato doesn't know how to create real emotional intimacy and thus only knows how to relate to those she cares about with sex. It's a shortcut that ultimately leads nowhere, because even when such openness is offered she's terrified of rejection and thus has to run away.

[]:             Quote Post by Killericon » Wed Feb 24, 2010 3:40 pm The dichotomy of her 'slutty' actions and her only pursuing one man is pretty important to how the show portrays her, I think. They deliberately did it that way. NemZ put it pretty perfectly.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Feb 24, 2010 4:10 pm NemZ wrote: That's because she's not a slut.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 24, 2010 4:47 pm Xard wrote: They're all sluts

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Wed Feb 24, 2010 6:12 pm Oh, but she was a slut. She was getting wasted and banging dudes (and some ladies) left and right, its just that we didn't see it on screen.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Wed Feb 24, 2010 6:42 pm She just fell into that trap of sex = intimacy/love even though sex is neither if the love isn't there also. Not sure, but isn't there evidence to suggest lack of a good father-figure for girls can make them slutty in later life because they don't know how else to behave around men so just do what society says attacts them? If so that's probably Misato's case, just she only did it with Kaji.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Feb 24, 2010 7:45 pm Misato is in no way a slut. Sure, she flirts a lot, but not in a way where she is seriously pining for Shinji's manhood; she does it in more of a fun, teasing way toward him. With Kaji, the only man in the entirety of the show that she has had a relationship with, it's different because they were, in fact, lovers. Her loving to have sex with her lover does not make her a slut, especially if she actually loved him, which all signs seem to point to. Calling Misato a slut is an even bigger stretch than calling Yoko Littner a slut, whom of which may dress suggestively during certain arcs of the show, but had only partook in romantic/sexual activities with two different men seven years apart from each other (and in the case of Kittan, it wasn't exactly mutual on her part); aside from those two affairs, Yoko was a conservatively dressed school teacher.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Wed Feb 24, 2010 8:41 pm ^Well, Gainax threatened to literally erase Yoko from existence if she didn't dress like that. Misato isn't a "slut" or whatever the less gender inclusive term would be. She's a sex fanatic, definitely. Many would be desensitized to sex after one straight week of it.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Wed Feb 24, 2010 8:57 pm In light of the recent re-definition of the term by Tiger Woods, allow me to note that she is a "sex addict".

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Wed Feb 24, 2010 9:11 pm ^And a "very bad person".

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Thu Feb 25, 2010 5:15 am Sachi wrote: Misato is in no way a slut. Sure, she flirts a lot, but not in a way where she is seriously pining for Shinji's manhood; she does it in more of a fun, teasing way toward him. With Kaji, the only man in the entirety of the show that she has had a relationship with, it's different because they were, in fact, lovers. Her loving to have sex with her lover does not make her a slut, especially if she actually loved him, which all signs seem to point to. Calling Misato a slut is an even bigger stretch than calling Yoko Littner a slut, whom of which may dress suggestively during certain arcs of the show, but had only partook in romantic/sexual activities with two different men seven years apart from each other (and in the case of Kittan, it wasn't exactly mutual on her part); aside from those two affairs, Yoko was a conservatively dressed school teacher.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Feb 25, 2010 8:47 am Halicat wrote: I don't understand how a week of sex with one person could make one a fanatic for it? Or even desensitize them to it?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Feb 26, 2010 5:21 am Mr. Tines wrote: Xard wrote: They're all sluts

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Fri Feb 26, 2010 8:03 am Sachi wrote: I believe he was saying that she's a sex addict who doesn't even get tired of, or desensitized after a single week of straight sex, which, for a lot of people, may get a little monotonous.

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Fri Feb 26, 2010 11:16 am Like in Re-take .

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Fri Feb 26, 2010 6:15 pm How did they show sex was her main character flaw? And just because someone has sex mean they offer it to every person they see. There were only a couple of guys in the show anyways.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Feb 27, 2010 12:34 pm And she had much bigger character flaws anyway, like her complete immaturity and lack of high-minded responsibility outside of her personal vendetta against the Angels, and her big ol' love/hate Daddy thing that's crippled her entire life.

[]:             Quote Post by BGMLV » Sat Feb 27, 2010 6:59 pm Being a slut is a very very VERY, Good thing!

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Feb 27, 2010 8:44 pm Misato seems flirty more than anything else. If she really was a slut I think it would have been more heavily implied in the show. BGMLV wrote: VERY, Good thing!
